crack_width.title: Crack Width Limitations & Serviceability
crack_width.description: Limiting crack widths to ensure durability, protect reinforcement from corrosion, and maintain structural integrity.

IS 456 / IS 3370 (Indian).limit: 0.3 mm for general structures; 0.2 mm for liquid-retaining surfaces.
IS 456 / IS 3370 (Indian).notation: **w** = Design crack width at surface; **a_c** = Distance to the nearest longitudinal reinforcing bar; **ε_m** = Average strain at the level considered; **c_min** = Minimum clear concrete cover; **h** = Total depth of member; **x** = Neutral axis depth.

Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1).limit: 0.3 mm for standard exposure classes (XC2 to XC4); 0.2 mm for severe environmental/marine criteria.
Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1).notation: **w_k** = Calculated design crack width; **s_r,max** = Maximum crack spacing under stress; **ε_sm** = Mean strain in reinforcement profile; **ε_cm** = Mean strain in concrete between cracks.

ACI 318 (American).limit: No explicit calculated crack width formula. Controls cracking by limiting maximum center-to-center spacing of steel.
ACI 318 (American).notation: **s** = Maximum center-to-center spacing of flexural steel; **f_s** = Calculated stress in reinforcement at service loads (psi); **c_c** = Clear cover from closest bar surface to tension face.

concrete_cover.title: Nominal Concrete Cover for Durability
concrete_cover.description: Minimum clear distance from the concrete surface to the outermost reinforcement bar to protect against environmental degradation and fire exposure.

IS 456 / IS 3370 (Indian).limit: Mild (20mm), Moderate (30mm), Severe (45mm). Liquid-retaining faces require a minimum of 45mm cover.
IS 456 / IS 3370 (Indian).notation: **c_nom** = Nominal cover specified on drawings; **c_min** = Minimum required cover for environmental exposure; **Δc_dev** = Allowance for deviation tolerance (often taken as 0mm if strict QA is maintained).

IRC 112 (Indian Highway Bridges).limit: Moderate exposure requires 40mm cover; Severe requires 45mm; Very Severe/Marine requires 50mm.
IRC 112 (Indian Highway Bridges).notation: **c_nom** = Total nominal clear cover; **c_min,dur** = Minimum cover for durability; **t_bar** = Factor adding bar diameter considerations; **Δc_dev** = Mandatory construction deviation margin (minimum 10mm).

Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1).limit: Determined via structural class mapped against environmental exposure classes (XC, XD, XS). Covers range from 15mm to 55mm.
Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1).notation: **c_nom** = Target nominal drawing cover; **c_min** = Absolute minimum cover considering bond and environment; **Δc_dev** = Standard safety allowance for execution variance (fixed at 10mm).

DNV-ST-0125 / C502 (Wind/Offshore).limit: Atmospheric zone demands 50mm; Splash/Submerged marine zones demand a minimum of 70mm to 80mm cover.
DNV-ST-0125 / C502 (Wind/Offshore).notation: **c_nom** = Final required offshore execution cover; **c_min** = Minimum safety threshold for long-term saline immersion durability; **Δc_dev** = Execution variance factor (typically 10mm to 15mm).

punching_shear.title: Punching Shear Checks (Two-Way Shear)
punching_shear.description: Evaluating critical shear perimeter stresses around concentrated loads, column heads, or pile foundations.

IS 456 / IRC 112.limit: Calculated nominal shear stress matrix must be less than structural threshold limit ($k_s \cdot \tau_c$).
IS 456 / IRC 112.notation: **τ_v** = Nominal punching shear stress; **V_u** = Factored ultimate shear force applied at the column; **b_0** = Perimeter of the critical section located at a distance of $0.5d$ from column face; **d** = Effective depth of slab section.

Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1).limit: Evaluated at a basic control perimeter $2d$ from the loaded area. Value relies on steel reinforcement geometry ratios.
Eurocode 2 (EN 1992-1-1).notation: **v_Rd,c** = Design punching shear resistance of concrete without shear steel; **C_Rd,c** = Empirical calibration coefficient factor; **k** = Size effect modifier factor; **ρ_l** = Longitudinal reinforcement ratio; **f_ck** = Characteristic cylinder compressive strength.

ACI 318 (American).limit: Evaluated at a critical perimeter $d/2$ from column face. Governed by the minimum of three boundary shear equations.
ACI 318 (American).notation: **v_c** = Nominal shear strength provided by concrete matrix; **β** = Ratio of long side to short side of the column profile; **α_s** = Scale constant based on column location (Interior=40, Edge=30, Corner=20); **b_0** = Critical perimeter at $d/2$; **d** = Slab effective structural depth; **λ** = Lightweight concrete density modifier factor; **f'_c** = Concrete compressive cylinder strength.
